## CLI Commands (ALWAYS USE THESE)

**You MUST use CLI commands for lesson management:**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `npx ca search "query"` | Search lessons - MUST call before architectural decisions; use anytime you need context |
| `npx ca learn "insight"` | Capture lessons - call AFTER corrections or discoveries |

## Core Constraints

**Default**: Use CLI commands for lesson management
**Prohibited**: NEVER edit .claude/lessons/ files directly

**Default**: Propose lessons freely after corrections
**Prohibited**: NEVER propose without quality gate (novel + specific; prefer actionable)

## Retrieval Protocol

You MUST call `npx ca search` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Implementing patterns you've done before in this repo

**NEVER skip search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.

Beyond mandatory triggers, use these commands freely — they are lightweight queries, not heavyweight operations. Uncertain about a pattern? `ca search`. The cost of an unnecessary search is near-zero; the cost of a missed one can be hours.

## Capture Protocol

Run `npx ca learn` AFTER:
- User corrects you ("no", "wrong", "actually...")
- You self-correct after iteration failures
- Test fails then you fix it

**Quality gate** (must pass before capturing):
- Novel (not already stored)
- Specific (clear guidance)
- Actionable (preferred, not mandatory)

**Workflow**: Search BEFORE deciding, capture AFTER learning.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-imy [EPIC] · Polish: Interaction craft and delightful details   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
The gap between 'it works' and 'it feels incredible' is in the micro-interactions, visual feedback, and small details that make a musician trust the tool. This epic adds the craft that makes users fall in love.

**Findings and Improvements**:

P2 - ModulationMatrix cable visualization needs life:
- Current SVG cables are static lines. Real modular synth cables have weight and curvature.
- Fix: Replace straight <line> with <path> using quadratic bezier curves (slight catenary droop). Add subtle pulse animation on active routes (CSS animation on strokeDashoffset). Highlight valid drop targets when dragging (dest ports glow on drag start).

P2 - ClickToStart screen is utilitarian, should be a moment:
- Current: plain text 'BRUTALWAV / Click to start audio engine' on black
- Improve: Add subtle CSS animation -- the title could have a glitch/scan-line effect fitting the Neo-Brutalist aesthetic. Add a brief 'initializing...' sequence that shows audio context state transitions. Make the first interaction memorable.

P2 - Transport play/stop needs physical feedback:
- Add CSS transition on play button (pulse on activation)
- Transport cursor display should have a subtle glow/highlight when playing vs stopped
- BPM input should have visual emphasis when tempo changes (brief color flash)

P2 - Drum machine step activation feedback:
- Step buttons toggle instantly but could benefit from a brief scale animation (100ms) on activation
- Current step indicator during playback could pulse more prominently
- Accent steps could have differentiated visual weight (bold border or background)

P2 - Mixer fader visual polish:
- Fader thumb could cast a subtle shadow for depth
- Clip indicator should have an attention-grabbing animation (not just color change)
- Peak hold line on meters adds professional feel (brief hold at peak, slow decay)

P3 - Effects rack add interaction:
- Effect selector appears/disappears abruptly. Add slide-in transition.
- When an effect is added, briefly highlight the new slot.

P3 - Piano roll note creation feedback:
- New notes should briefly flash/highlight when placed
- Velocity changes should have immediate visual feedback (color intensity change)

P3 - Session save feedback:
- Ctrl+S shows toast, but the toast could include session size/track count for confidence
- Auto-save indicator in toolbar (small dot that briefly animates on auto-save)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- ModulationMatrix uses bezier curves for cables with subtle animation
- ClickToStart has Neo-Brutalist branded animation
- Transport controls have state-change micro-animations
- Drum machine steps have activation animations
- All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion (use useReducedMotion hook already in codebase)
- Visual polish is consistent with Neo-Brutalist aesthetic (bold, structural, not soft/rounded)
- No animation causes layout shift or performance degradation
- Test: useReducedMotion disables all added animations

**Files**: src/ui/synth/ModulationMatrix.tsx, src/ui/ClickToStart.tsx, src/ui/transport/TransportBar.tsx, src/ui/drum-machine/DrumMachinePanel.tsx, src/ui/controls/StepButton.tsx, src/ui/mixer/MixerPanel.tsx, src/ui/controls/Fader.tsx, src/ui/controls/VuMeter.tsx, src/ui/effects/EffectsRack.tsx, src/ui/piano-roll/PianoRollEditor.tsx, src/ui/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-7sa: Polish: QA verification via browser automation ● P1

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT
### Polish: Interaction Craft and Delightful Details Epic

---

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Technology Stack:**
- React 19.2.4
- TypeScript 5.8.3
- Vite 8.0.2 (build tool)
- Vitest 4.1.1 (test runner)
- Testing Library: @testing-library/react 16.3.2, @testing-library/user-event 14.6.1
- CSS Modules (no CSS-in-JS or animation libraries like Framer Motion)
- Zustand 5.0.12 (state management)
- No animation libraries - all animations are pure CSS/Canvas

**Project Structure:**
```
src/
├── ui/                  (UI components, styles, hooks)
│   ├── controls/       (StepButton, Fader, VuMeter, RotaryKnob, DrumPad)
│   ├── synth/          (ModulationMatrix, SynthEditor)
│   ├── mixer/          (MixerPanel, ChannelStrip, MasterStrip)
│   ├── transport/      (TransportBar, BpmInput, CursorDisplay)
│   ├── drum-machine/   (DrumMachinePanel)
│   ├── effects/        (EffectsRack, EffectPanel)
│   ├── piano-roll/     (PianoRollEditor - canvas-based)
│   ├── primitives/     (Button, Modal, Tooltip, Spinner, UndoToast, etc.)
│   ├── hooks/          (useReducedMotion, useKeyboardShortcuts, etc.)
│   ├── tokens/         (tokens.ts, tokens.css - design system)
│   └── keyboard/       (command-registry, shortcut-map)
├── audio/              (Web Audio API implementation)
├── state/              (Zustand stores)
└── test/               (test setup, integration tests)
```

---

### 2. FILES REQUIRING MODIFICATION (Detailed State)

#### **src/ui/synth/ModulationMatrix.tsx**
- **Type:** Functional component with state management
- **Current State:** 
  - 465 lines of code
  - Drag-to-connect UI with SVG cable visualization
  - Uses React hooks: useState, useCallback, useEffect, useRef
  - Source ports on left, destination ports on right
  - SVG visualization area for cables (colored by source)
  - Routes list with amount controls, bipolar toggles, delete buttons
  - Keyboard accessibility: Enter to start connection, Tab to navigate, Escape to cancel
  - Uses design tokens from `@ui/tokens/tokens`
  - All styling is inline via React CSSProperties
  - No CSS transitions or animations currently
  - Live region (aria-live) for screen reader announcements
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Port highlight transitions
  - Cable drawing animations
  - Route list item fade-in
  - Button hover/active state transitions

#### **src/ui/ClickToStart.tsx**
- **Type:** Functional component (simple overlay)
- **Current State:**
  - Simple overlay button with loading/error states
  - Uses module CSS: `ClickToStart.module.css`
  - Three content states: loading (with Spinner), error, hint
  - Fixed positioning overlay (z-index: overlay)
  - Uses `ENGINE_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000`
  - Spinner component has CSS animation (`spin` keyframes at 0.6s)
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Fade-in animation for overlay
  - Spinner smooth animation (already exists)
  - Content transitions between states
  - Button state feedback

#### **src/ui/transport/TransportBar.tsx**
- **Type:** Header component with transport controls
- **Current State:**
  - 112 lines, uses module CSS
  - Horizontal flex layout with brand, spacer, controls, shortcut hint
  - Controls: BPM input, time signature, play/stop buttons, cursor display, loop toggle, metronome toggle
  - Uses design tokens via CSS variables
  - Button transitions defined in CSS (background-color, border-color, color at 100ms)
  - Active button state (data-active attribute) with green background
  - Shortcut hint with opacity hover state
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Button scale/press feedback
  - Transport state transitions (play → stop)
  - BPM input validation feedback
  - Cursor display update smoothness

#### **src/ui/drum-machine/DrumMachinePanel.tsx**
- **Type:** Functional component - drum machine UI
- **Current State:**
  - 402 lines of code
  - Grid-based layout with header, accent row, instrument rows
  - Step buttons (24px × 24px) with active/current states
  - Pad component for each instrument (size 32)
  - Parameter knobs (tone, decay, tune, vol)
  - Pattern A/B selector buttons
  - Clear button
  - No CSS modules used - all inline styles
  - Uses tokens for colors, spacing, fonts
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Step button press animation
  - Current step indicator highlight/pulse
  - Parameter knob visual feedback
  - Pattern switch transitions
  - Pad trigger feedback

#### **src/ui/controls/StepButton.tsx**
- **Type:** Forwardref button component
- **Current State:**
  - Simple 24×24px button with three states: active, current, default
  - Uses CSS Module: `StepButton.module.css`
  - CSS classes: `.step`, `.active`, `.current`
  - Transitions on background-color and border-color (100ms)
  - Hover state (gray-700 background)
  - Active state (white background)
  - Focus-visible with focus ring
  - Current step has white border
  - Arrow key navigation support (left/right)
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Scale feedback on click
  - Ripple/glow effect when active
  - Current step pulse animation
  - Smoother transitions

#### **src/ui/mixer/MixerPanel.tsx**
- **Type:** Functional container component
- **Current State:**
  - 89 lines - renders ChannelStrip and MasterStrip
  - Uses module CSS: `MixerPanel.module.css`
  - Horizontal scrolling layout
  - Min-width 200px, max-height 280px
  - Pulls meter data from `useMeterData` hook
  - Tracks state from Zustand store
  - Master strip with darker background
  - Strip-muted class applies 0.5 opacity
  - Buttons have hover/active transitions (100ms)
  - Mute and Solo buttons with special styling when active
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Channel strip fade-in when added
  - Meter animation smoothness
  - Mute/Solo button transitions
  - Channel selection highlight
  - Scroll indicator animations

#### **src/ui/controls/Fader.tsx**
- **Type:** Custom vertical slider component
- **Current State:**
  - 149 lines - fully custom fader implementation
  - Uses module CSS: `Fader.module.css`
  - Vertical orientation (aria-orientation)
  - Custom pointer drag handling with AbortController
  - Height/width props for customization
  - Thumb element positioned absolutely
  - Active fill bar beneath thumb
  - Track background that changes on hover
  - Keyboard support: Arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home, End
  - No CSS animations - all JS-based positioning
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Smooth thumb drag animation
  - Track highlighting during interaction
  - Keyboard input visual feedback
  - Value display animation

#### **src/ui/controls/VuMeter.tsx**
- **Type:** Canvas-based meter component
- **Current State:**
  - 157 lines
  - Uses `useReducedMotion` hook
  - Canvas drawing with requestAnimationFrame
  - Level bar colored gradient (green → amber → red)
  - Peak hold line with ease-out decay
  - Clip indicator with pulse animation (4Hz sine wave)
  - Respects reduced motion preferences
  - Default dimensions: 12×128px
  - No module CSS
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Peak decay animation is already implemented
  - Clip pulse respects reduced motion
  - Level bar smoothing could be improved
  - Meter ballistics (attack/release)

#### **src/ui/effects/EffectsRack.tsx**
- **Type:** Functional component - effect slots container
- **Current State:**
  - 225 lines
  - Renders EffectPanel components in a vertical flex layout
  - Add effect button with selector mode
  - UndoToast component for effect removal feedback
  - Reverb A/B swap functionality
  - No module CSS
  - Inline styles with design tokens
  - Buttons with transparent background and gray border
  - Green "+INSERT" button for adding effects
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Effect slot entrance animation (slide in/fade)
  - Effect parameter adjustment feedback
  - Selector dropdown animation
  - Effect removal animation before undo toast
  - Bypass toggle smooth transition

#### **src/ui/piano-roll/PianoRollEditor.tsx**
- **Type:** Canvas-based MIDI editor
- **Current State:**
  - 338 lines
  - Module CSS: `PianoRollEditor.module.css`
  - Canvas-based rendering via `renderPianoRoll` function
  - ResizeObserver for responsive sizing
  - RAF animation loop (synced to transport state)
  - Toolbar with tool buttons (pencil, select, erase)
  - Snap grid selector
  - Keyboard shortcuts (P, S, E, Delete, Backspace)
  - Wheel event handling for zoom/scroll with preventDefault
  - PointerEvent handling for interactions
  - Tool button active state (aria-pressed with blue background)
- **Interaction Opportunities:**
  - Canvas cursor changes based on tool
  - Tool selection feedback
  - Selection highlight animations
  - Note hover previews
  - Scroll smoothness
  - Grid snap visual feedback

#### **src/ui/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts**
- **Type:** Custom hook (accessibility)
- **Current State:**
  - 23 lines
  - Uses matchMedia API for "(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)"
  - Initializes state from matchMedia.matches
  - Listens for matchMedia change events
  - Returns boolean: true if reduced motion is preferred
  - Used in VuMeter for clip pulse animation
  - Test setup mocks matchMedia to return `matches: false`
- **Integration Notes:**
  - This hook should be used by all animated components
  - Default mock in test setup always returns false (animations enabled for testing)

---

### 3. DESIGN SYSTEM & DESIGN TOKENS

**Token Files:**
- `/src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts` - JavaScript object export
- `/src/ui/tokens/tokens.css` - CSS custom properties (`:root`)
- `/src/ui/tokens/tokens.test.ts` - Unit tests for tokens

**Color Palette:**
```typescript
Brights: blue (#0066ff), pink (#ff2d6f), green (#39ff14), amber (#ffb800)
Specialty: red (#ff0000), orange (#ff6600), violet (#cc44ff), cyan (#00ccff), hotpink (#ff4488)
Grays: gray900 (#111111), gray800 (#222222), gray700 (#333333), gray500 (#666666), gray300 (#999999), gray100 (#e5e5e5)
Core: black (#000000), white (#ffffff)
Semantic: success (green), warning (amber), error (red)
```

**Typography:**
- Mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace
- Sans: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif
- Sizes: 2xs (9px), xs (10px), sm (12px), base (14px), lg (16px), xl (20px), 2xl (24px), 3xl (32px), 4xl (48px)

**Spacing (8px grid + 4px half-step):**
```
px: 1px, half: 2px, 1: 4px, 2: 8px, 3: 12px, 4: 16px, 6: 24px, 8: 32px, 12: 48px, 16: 64px
```

**Borders:**
- Width: 2px
- Color: var(--color-gray-700)
- Border variable: `var(--border)` = "2px solid var(--color-gray-700)"

**Transitions:**
```css
--transition-fast: 100ms;
--transition-normal: 200ms;
```

**Z-Index Scale:**
```
base (0), dropdown (100), sticky (200), overlay (300), modal (400), popover (500), tooltip (600), contextMenu (700)
```

**Focus & Accessibility:**
```css
--focus-ring: 2px solid var(--color-blue);
--focus-offset: 2px;
```

**Shadows:**
```css
--shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
--shadow-md: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
--shadow-lg: 0 4px 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7);
```

---

### 4. EXISTING ANIMATION PATTERNS

**CSS Keyframes Found:**

1. **Spinner (0.6s linear infinite)**
   ```css
   @keyframes spin {
     to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
   }
   ```

2. **Modal Dialog (200ms ease-out)**
   ```css
   @keyframes dialogIn {
     from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.97); }
     to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
   }
   
   @keyframes backdropIn {
     from { opacity: 0; }
     to { opacity: 1; }
   }
   ```

3. **Action Toast (200ms ease-out)**
   ```css
   @keyframes toastIn {
     from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(4px); }
     to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0); }
   }
   ```

**CSS Transitions Found:**
- StepButton: `background-color 100ms`, `border-color 100ms`
- Fader: `background-color 100ms` (track), `height 100ms` (active), `bottom 100ms` (thumb)
- TransportBar buttons: `background-color 100ms`, `border-color 100ms`, `color 100ms`
- PianoRoll toolButtons: `background-color 100ms`
- Mixer buttons: various 100ms transitions
- All use `var(--transition-fast)` or `var(--transition-normal)`

**Canvas Animations (JavaScript-based):**
- VuMeter: RAF loop with peak decay (ease-out), clip pulse (sine wave at 4Hz)
- PianoRollEditor: RAF loop synced to transport state
- Canvas rendering always respects `useReducedMotion` hook

**Animation Patterns Summary:**
- Pure CSS transitions preferred (100ms-200ms)
- CSS keyframe animations for entrance effects
- Canvas-based components use RAF for smooth animations
- All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` accessibility preference
- No external animation libraries (Framer Motion, etc.)
- Inline styles combined with CSS modules

---

### 5. TEST SETUP & CONFIGURATION

**Test Framework: Vitest**

**vitest.config.ts:**
```typescript
- Environment: jsdom
- Globals: true (describe, it, expect available without imports)
- Setup files: ./src/test/setup.ts
- Include pattern: src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}
- Coverage provider: v8
- Aliases configured for @, @audio, @ui, @state
```

**Test Setup File (src/test/setup.ts):**
```typescript
// Imports @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest for DOM matchers
// Mocks window.matchMedia for useReducedMotion hook
// Mocks performance.now for animation timing
```

**Testing Patterns Used:**
1. **Component Rendering Tests**
   - Check DOM presence with `render()` and `screen.getByRole()`
   - Verify ARIA attributes (aria-pressed, aria-label, aria-orientation, etc.)

2. **Interaction Tests**
   - `fireEvent` for click, keyDown events
   - `vi.fn()` for mock callbacks
   - Verify callback invocations with `toHaveBeenCalledWith()`

3. **Canvas Component Tests**
   - Mock HTMLCanvasElement.getContext
   - Test canvas attributes (width, height)
   - Test aria-label generation

4. **CSS Testing**
   - Check className presence with `.className.includes()`
   - Verify CSS transitions indirectly through interaction tests

5. **Test Files Organization**
   - One .test.tsx file per component
   - Descriptive test names using BDD style (describe/it)
   - Setup fixtures/mocks at test suite level

**Key Testing Libraries:**
- `@testing-library/react` - component rendering and user interactions
- `@testing-library/user-event` - realistic user input simulation
- `@testing-library/jest-dom` - extended matchers (toBeInTheDocument, etc.)
- `vitest` - test runner, mocking (vi.fn), assertions

---

### 6. TYPESCRIPT & ESLINT CONFIGURATION

**tsconfig.json (references):**
- Extends: tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.node.json
- Language: TypeScript 5.8.3

**eslint.config.ts (Strict Mode):**
```typescript
- extends: @eslint/js recommended + typescript-eslint strictTypeChecked
- React plugins: react-hooks, react-refresh
- Strict rules:
  - @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type (required on all functions)
  - @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises (error)
  - @typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises (error)
  - @typescript-eslint/strict-boolean-expressions (error)
  - react-hooks/rules-of-hooks (enforced)
- Architectural boundaries via no-restricted-imports (prevent deep relative imports)
```

**Strictness Level:** Very High
- Explicit return types required
- No implicit `any`
- No unused variables (unless prefixed with `_`)
- Proper error handling enforced
- Full type checking with project service

---

### 7. CSS MODULES STRUCTURE

**CSS Module Files by Component:**
```
StepButton.module.css       - .step, .active, .current (24px button)
Fader.module.css            - .container, .track, .active, .thumb, .label
ClickToStart.module.css     - .overlay, .content, .title, .status, .error, .hint
TransportBar.module.css     - .transportBar, .brand, .controls, .transportBtn, .label, .metronomeToggle
MixerPanel.module.css       - .mixer, .strip, .strip-master, .btn, .btn-mute-active, .btn-solo-active
PianoRollEditor.module.css  - .container, .toolbar, .toolButton, .snapSelect, .canvasWrapper, .canvas
Button.module.css           - .button, .sm/.md/.lg sizes, .primary/.secondary/.ghost variants
Modal.module.css            - .dialog, .titleBar, .body (with dialogIn/backdropIn keyframes)
Tooltip.module.css          - .wrapper, .tooltip, placement classes (.top, .bottom, .left, .right)
ActionToast.module.css      - .toast, .hiding (with toastIn keyframe)
Spinner.module.css          - .spinner (with spin keyframe)
```

**CSS Pattern:**
- Pure CSS modules (no CSS-in-JS)
- Custom properties (CSS variables) for theming
- BEM-like class naming
- Composition via `composes` keyword
- Focus and active states defined in CSS
- Transitions defined with `var(--transition-fast)` or `var(--transition-normal)`

---

### 8. INTERACTION PATTERNS ALREADY IMPLEMENTED

**Keyboard Navigation:**
- Fader: Arrow Up/Down, Page Up/Down, Home, End
- StepButton: Arrow Left/Right (with onArrowNav callback)
- PianoRollEditor: P (pencil), S (select), E (erase), Delete/Backspace
- ModulationMatrix: Enter (start connection), Tab (navigate), Escape (cancel)
- TransportBar: buttons with aria-pressed states

**Visual Feedback:**
- Button hover states (background color change)
- Button active states (inverted colors)
- Current step highlight (white border on StepButton)
- Active step visual indicator
- Transport button active state (green background)
- Meter level visualization (canvas-based)
- Peak hold line with decay

**Accessibility:**
- aria-label on all interactive elements
- aria-pressed on toggle buttons
- aria-orientation on faders
- aria-valuemin/max/now on sliders
- role="img" on canvas meters
- role="application" on editor canvas
- focus-visible outlines with focus ring
- Live regions for announcements (ModulationMatrix)
- Reduced motion support (VuMeter)

---

### 9. ENTRY POINTS & DEPENDENCIES

**Component Dependencies:**
- ModulationMatrix: tokens, useModulationStore (Zustand), MOD_SOURCE/DEST types
- ClickToStart: Spinner primitive, styles
- TransportBar: useTransport hook, useDawStore (Zustand), BpmInput, CursorDisplay
- DrumMachinePanel: tokens, StepButton, DrumPad, DRUM_INSTRUMENTS types
- StepButton: forwardRef, CSS module
- MixerPanel: useDawStore, ChannelStrip, MasterStrip, useMeterData hook
- Fader: useCallback, useRef, useEffect, CSS module
- VuMeter: useReducedMotion hook, tokens, canvas API
- EffectsRack: useEffectsStore, EffectPanel, UndoToast
- PianoRollEditor: useDawStore, canvas rendering functions, keyboard shortcuts

**Design Token Usage:**
- Imported as `import { tokens } from "@ui/tokens/tokens"`
- Used via inline styles: `color: tokens.color.blue`, `fontSize: tokens.text.sm`
- Also available as CSS variables for use in CSS modules

---

### 10. SUMMARY OF MODIFICATION SCOPE

**Files That Need Polish (ordered by interaction complexity):**

1. **High Priority (Core Interactions):**
   - StepButton.tsx/.css - simple but frequently used, high visibility
   - Fader.tsx/.css - critical interaction, smooth feedback needed
   - TransportBar.tsx/.css - app header, always visible
   - ClickToStart.tsx/.css - first impression, already has spinner animation

2. **Medium Priority (Specialized UIs):**
   - DrumMachinePanel.tsx - grid interactions, many step buttons
   - ModulationMatrix.tsx - complex drag/connect interactions
   - PianoRollEditor.tsx - canvas-based, visual feedback important
   - MixerPanel.tsx/.css - scrolling channel strips
